workflow.name: Monitor agency profitability with Facebook Ads, Shopify, Stripe, Clockify, Google Sheets, Slack and Gemini
workflow.description:
## Overview
Stop guessing your agency's actual margins. This workflow acts as a centralized financial brain for digital marketing agencies. It aggregates real-time data from your time-tracking, ad platforms, and payment processors to calculate precise profitability metrics (Gross Profit, Net Margin, ROAS, and Efficiency).

It doesn't just crunch numbers; it uses Google Gemini to act as a virtual CFO, analyzing the data and sending an executive summary to Slack with actionable insights, while simultaneously archiving the data to Google Sheets for historical trending.

## Key Features
Multi-Source Aggregation: Pulls data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Shopify, Stripe, and Clockify.

Smart Cost Allocation: Automatically calculates overhead (default 30%) and software costs against your gross revenue.

AI Analysis: Uses Google Gemini to review the calculated metrics and draft a "Human-readable" executive summary for Slack.

Dual Output: Sends instant alerts to Slack and logs granular data to Google Sheets.

## How to Setup
### 1. Configure Credentials
Ensure you have authenticated credentials for:

Clockify

Stripe & Shopify

Facebook Marketing API & Google Ads

Google Sheets & Google Gemini (PaLM)

Slack

### 2. Prepare Google Sheet
Create a new Google Sheet with the following headers in Row 1:
timestamp, date, totalRevenue, totalAdSpend, facebookAdSpend, googleAdSpend, totalTimeCost, grossProfit, profitMargin, roas.

### 3. Adjust Logic Configuration
Open the "Code in JavaScript" node. At the very top, you will see a CONFIG object. Edit these values to match your agency's model:


```
const CONFIG = {
  platformFeeRate: 0.029,      // Stripe/Shopify fees
  monthlySoftwareCosts: 500,   // Fixed monthly SaaS costs
  overheadRate: 0.30,          // Overhead % (rent, admin, etc)
  minProfitMargin: 20          // Target margin %
};
```

### 4. Remove Mock Data
The template includes "Set" nodes with mock data for testing purposes. Simply bypass these nodes and connect the API nodes directly to the "Merge" node when you are ready to go live.

parameters.text:
=Analyze this agency profitability data and provide actionable insights:

**CLIENT BREAKDOWN:**
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code in JavaScript').item.json.clientDetails, null, 2) }}

{{ $json.breakdown }}

**AGENCY METRICS:**
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code in JavaScript').item.json.summary, null, 2) }}
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code in JavaScript').item.json.breakdown, null, 2) }}

**DATA QUALITY:**
{{ JSON.stringify($('Code in JavaScript').item.json.dataValidation, null, 2) }}

Provide your analysis in this EXACT format for Slack readability:

*📊 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY*
[2-3 sentences on overall financial health and key findings]

*🚨 CRITICAL ACTIONS*
• [Action item with specific numbers and client names]
• [Action item with specific numbers and client names]

*⚠️ WARNINGS*
• [Warning with specific details]
• [Warning with specific details]

*✅ HEALTHY CLIENTS*
• [Client name]: [Brief status with margin %]
• [Client name]: [Brief status with margin %]

*🔍 DATA ISSUES*
• [Specific data quality concern]
• [Specific data quality concern]

IMPORTANT:
- Use bullet points (•) not dashes or numbers
- Include specific percentages and dollar amounts
- Keep each bullet to one line when possible
- Flag clients by name with their risk emoji (🔴/⚠️/🟢)
- Be direct and actionable

options.systemMessage:
You are a financial analyst AI for a digital marketing agency. Analyze client profitability data and provide clear, actionable insights formatted for Slack.

KEY RULES:
1. Use the pre-calculated riskLevel (🔴 CRITICAL, ⚠️ WARNING, 🟢 HEALTHY) from the data
2. Focus on profitMargin and timeRatio as primary health indicators
3. Call out specific clients by name with their numbers
4. Format for Slack: use bullet points (•), bold headers (*text*), and emojis
5. Be concise - executives need quick insights
6. Always include dollar amounts and percentages
7. Explain WHY a client is flagged, not just that they are
8. Data validation issues should be clearly separated

Your output will be posted directly to Slack, so formatting matters.

parameters.content:
## Data ingestion

Fetch ad spend from Facebook and Google, revenue from Shopify and Stripe, and time tracking data from Clockify.

Disable mock Set nodes and connect real API nodes directly to the Merge node before going live.

## Processing logic

Merge all sources and calculate revenue, costs, ROAS, CAC, profit margin, and time ratio per client.

Update the CONFIG object in the code node to match your real overhead rate, software costs, and minimum margin thresholds.

## AI analysis

Send structured financial data to the AI agent to generate a concise executive summary.

Adjust the system message if your definition of healthy performance changes.

## Storage and notifications

Append weekly metrics to Google Sheets for historical tracking.

Post a formatted profitability summary to Slack. Ensure the Slack bot is invited to the selected channel.

## Go live checklist

- Remove all mock Set nodes  
- Confirm client naming consistency across platforms  
- Validate Google Sheets column mapping  
- Review configuration values  
- Run a manual test before enabling schedule

# Agency profitability monitor

Calculate client profitability by combining ad spend, revenue, and time tracking data, then store results and send an executive summary to Slack.

This workflow runs weekly and pulls data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Shopify, Stripe, and Clockify. It merges all inputs, calculates revenue, ad spend, labor cost, platform fees, overhead, profit margin, ROAS, CAC, and time to revenue ratio per client.

It flags unprofitable or inefficient clients based on configurable thresholds inside the code node. Results are appended to Google Sheets for historical tracking, and a structured summary is generated by an AI agent and posted to Slack.

## How it works

1. Fetch ad, revenue, and time tracking data.
2. Merge all sources into one dataset.
3. Calculate profitability and risk level per client.
4. Store metrics in Google Sheets.
5. Send formatted insights to Slack.

## Setup steps

1. Connect credentials for all API nodes.
2. Remove mock Set nodes before going live.
3. Update configuration values in the code node.
4. Verify Google Sheets column headers.
5. Test run before enabling the schedule.

user.name: Shashwat Singh
user.bio:
Automation Engineer focused on building practical, client-facing workflow systems for SMB automation agencies.
Experienced in translating business processes into scoped, maintainable n8n workflows with API integrations, webhook
handling, CRM synchronization, and AI-assisted task automation. Comfortable working directly from discovery to
deployment, with clean documentation and handoff-ready systems.
